samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of the FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of the FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instance
        <instance id="dual_port_RAM" module_id="dual_port_RAM">
            <connection id="wclk" value="wclk"/>
            <connection id="wenc" value="wen"/>
            <connection id="waddr" value="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <connection id="wdata" value="wdata"/>
            <connection id="rclk" value="rclk"/>
            <connection id="renc" value="ren"/>
            <connection id="raddr" value="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <connection id="rdata" value="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <description>Binary representation of the write address.</description>
            <initial_value>0</initial_value>
            <always_block>
                <condition>posedge wclk or negedge wrstn</condition>
                <statement>
                    if (!wrstn) begin
                        waddr_bin <= 0;
                    end else if (winc && !wfull) begin
                        waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                    end
                </statement>
            </always_block>
        </reg>
        
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <description>Binary representation of the read address.</description>
            <initial_value>0</initial_value>
            <always_block>
                <condition>posedge rclk or negedge rrstn</condition>
                <statement>
                    if (!rrstn) begin
                        raddr_bin <= 0;
                    end else if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                        raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                    end
                </statement>
            </always_block>
        </reg>
        
        // Pointer buffers for synchronization
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <description>Buffer for write pointer.</description>
            <initial_value>0</initial_value>
            <always_block>
                <condition>posedge wclk or negedge wrstn</condition>
                <statement>
                    if (!wrstn) begin
                        wptr_buff <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        wptr_buff <= wptr;
                    end
                </statement>
            </always_block>
        </reg>
        
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <description>Buffer for read pointer.</description>
            <initial_value>0</initial_value>
            <always_block>
                <condition>posedge rclk or negedge rrstn</condition>
                <statement>
                    if (!rrstn) begin
                        rptr_buff <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        rptr_buff <= rptr;
                    end
                </statement>
            </always_block>
        </reg>
        
        // Full and empty flags
        <wire id="wfull">
            <description>Flag indicating if the FIFO is full.</description>
            <assign>wfull = (wptr == {~rptr[3], rptr[2:0]});</assign>
        </wire>
        
        <wire id="rempty">
            <description>Flag indicating if the FIFO is empty.</description>
            <assign>rempty = (rptr == wptr);</assign>
        </wire>
        
        // Assignments for dual-port RAM
        <assign>wen = winc && !wfull;</assign>
        <assign>ren = rinc && !rempty;</assign>
        <assign>rdata = ram_out;</assign>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Implements an asynchronous FIFO with dual-port RAM and pointer management.</implementation>
</module>
